headline: Amazon confirmation cycle. NLRB bargaining order (April 2 2026) confirmed as standing within window. Amazon has filed federal court appeal — Teamsters characterize this as exhausting Amazon's NLRB internal appeal options. Four-year anniversary of original ALU vote reached. AWS confirmed in Pentagon classified AI cohort. No new material entity-specific structural events beyond prior cycle scoring. Sub-threshold confirmation; band Critical sustained.
key_evidence: NLRB bargaining order April 2 2026: Amazon must negotiate with ALU-IBT Local 1 at JFK8.
Amazon confirmed federal court appeal; NLRB asked to move challenge to Second Circuit.
Four-year anniversary of original ALU vote (April 1 2022) reached.
AWS confirmed among 7-firm Pentagon classified AI deployment cohort.
Teamsters characterize federal appeal as exhausting Amazon's NLRB internal appeal options.

evidence.finding: NLRB bargaining order: Teamsters become first union to win bargaining order against Amazon.
NLRB bargaining order standing; Amazon will appeal in federal court.
Federal court appeal in progress; NLRB requested transfer to Second Circuit.

scoring_rationale.EMP: 1.5 unchanged. NLRB bargaining order already scored prior cycle.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 1.8 unchanged. Federal appeal is process update, not new evidence-class.
scoring_rationale.BND: 1.8 unchanged. AWS Pentagon participation already in evidence base.
scoring_rationale.INT: 1.6 unchanged. I1 Consistency Under Pressure: federal appeal posture indicates Amazon escalating rather than complying — but escalation already factored in prior cycle's anti-union stance assessment.

math_check.validator_check: Zero delta with minor rounding drift; Critical band sustained.

negativeSignals: Federal court appeal of NLRB order; AWS Pentagon classified AI participation; four-year delay since original ALU vote.
positiveSignals: Teamsters' continuing organizing capacity; NLRB external accountability mechanism functioning.
sector_context: Amazon's labor-relations posture is the most prolonged Fortune 500 anti-union case in active record. Federal-court appeal is a continuation of a multi-year delay strategy.
open_actions_resolved: Verified prior May 1 assessment captured April 2 NLRB ruling
Federal court appeal scored as continuation, not new evidence class
open_actions_carried: Monitor federal court appeal docket for material rulings
AWS Pentagon AI deal: BND assessment ongoing
